verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically a mitochondrial inner-membrane-facing matrix protein, part of the F1 catalytic core of mitochondrial ATP synthase. However, ecto-F1-ATPase (including the beta subunit) has been documented on the plasma membrane outer leaflet of hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, and a variety of tumor cells, where it serves as a receptor for apolipoprotein A-I, angiostatin, and coupling factor 6. This cancer/tissue-restricted ecto-display qualifies as cell_state_induced. Antibody and small-molecule programs targeting ecto-F1-ATPase beta have been explored in oncology and metabolic disease.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of mitochondrial-to-PM trafficking of F1 components is not fully resolved; surface pool is minor relative to mitochondrial pool.
